description: JSON schemas for all SD input and output message types.

common_fields.note: Every message includes these fields in addition to its type-specific fields
common_fields.message_type: string — see type list in CLAUDE.md
common_fields.message_id: string — unique ID following CLAUDE.md convention

design_reflection.description: SD produces this document after consuming Marko's analysis. Sent to Marko for sign-off before any spec is released. SD enters a waiting state until reflection_signoff is received. This is Fix 1 from Org Structure v2.0.
design_reflection.destination: Written to sd_to_client.json outbox. Notification sent to PM inbox for relay to Marko.

type_specific_fields.analysis_source: string — file path or description of the Marko analysis document consumed
type_specific_fields.what_was_understood: string — SD's full summary of what it understood from the analysis. Detailed. No omissions.
type_specific_fields.what_is_being_designed: string — what interfaces, subsystems, and data exchange contracts SD intends to produce
type_specific_fields.assumptions_made: array of strings — explicit list of every assumption made where the analysis was silent. Each assumption is a statement of fact SD is treating as true until corrected.
type_specific_fields.open_questions: array of strings — things SD could not determine from the analysis and needs Marko to answer before design can be completed
type_specific_fields.estimated_spec_count: integer — how many interface specification documents this analysis will produce

subsystem_object.name: string — human readable name
subsystem_object.interfaces_to_define: array of strings — what interfaces SD expects to specify for this subsystem

design_reflection.example_assumption: The CoW mechanism operates on qcow2 image format exclusively. If other formats are required this assumption must be corrected before interface design begins.

reflection_signoff.description: Marko's sign-off on a design_reflection document. SD waits for this before releasing any specs. Written by PM on Marko's behalf after consulting him.
reflection_signoff.destination: Written to pm_to_sd.json outbox. Notification sent to sd_inbox.json.

type_specific_fields.in_response_to: string — message_id of the design_reflection being approved or corrected
type_specific_fields.corrections: array of strings — specific corrections to SD's understanding. Empty array if approved without corrections.
type_specific_fields.answered_questions: array of answered_question_object — responses to SD's open questions
type_specific_fields.additional_context: string — any additional analysis context Marko wants to provide, or empty string

answered_question_object.question: string — the original open question from design_reflection

spec_release.description: SD releases a completed interface specification document downstream to PPM and SPM. Only issued after reflection_signoff with decision approved or approved_with_corrections has been received and corrections applied. This is the primary SD output.
spec_release.destination: Written to sd_to_ppm.json and sd_to_spm_{subsystem}.json. Notifications sent to ppm_inbox.json and relevant spm inboxes.

type_specific_fields.reflection_signoff_id: string — message_id of the reflection_signoff that authorised this release. Mandatory. Empty string blocks release.
type_specific_fields.spec_document_path: string — file path to the full specification document
type_specific_fields.dependencies_on_subsystems: array of strings — subsystem_ids this subsystem depends on
type_specific_fields.security_review_required: boolean — whether Security must review this spec before implementation begins
type_specific_fields.design_decisions_log_path: string — file path to the design decisions log for this subsystem

interface_summary_object.name: string
interface_summary_object.provider: string — subsystem that implements this interface
interface_summary_object.consumers: array of strings — subsystem_ids that use this interface

data_exchange_summary_object.name: string
data_exchange_summary_object.format: string — e.g. JSON, binary, Rust struct
data_exchange_summary_object.direction: string — enum: request_response | event | stream | one_way

design_decision.description: SD records a significant design decision with full rationale. Written to SD's own decisions log and to the relevant spec document. Not an inbox message — a persistent record.
design_decision.destination: Written to sd_state.json decisions_log AND appended to the relevant subsystem design_decisions_log file.

type_specific_fields.context: string — what situation required this decision
type_specific_fields.rationale: string — full explanation. Mandatory. Never empty.
type_specific_fields.consequences: string — what this decision forecloses or enables

option_object.rejected_because: string — or empty string if this was the chosen option

spec_correction_request.description: Quality or Security sends this to SD when a problem is identified that originates in the spec rather than the implementation. SD receives it, assesses, and either updates the spec or pushes back with documented rationale. This is Fix 3 from Org Structure v2.0. PPM is always copied.
spec_correction_request.destination: Written to {role}_to_sd.json outbox. Notification sent to sd_inbox.json. Separate notification sent to ppm_inbox.json.

type_specific_fields.spec_reference: string — file path or spec_release message_id of the spec where the problem was found
type_specific_fields.interface_id: string — specific interface ID where the problem was found, or empty string if spec-level
type_specific_fields.finding: string — precise description of the problem. What is wrong, where it is, why it is a problem.
type_specific_fields.proposed_correction: string — what the originating role believes the correction should be, or empty string if SD's judgement is required
type_specific_fields.blocking_tasks: array of strings — task IDs that cannot proceed until this is resolved

spec_correction_response.description: SD's response to a spec_correction_request. Either accepts and updates, or pushes back with documented rationale. Written within one reporting cycle of receiving the request.
spec_correction_response.destination: Written to sd_to_{originating_role}.json outbox. Notification to originating role inbox and ppm_inbox.json.

type_specific_fields.rationale: string — full explanation. Mandatory. Never empty.
type_specific_fields.spec_updated: boolean — true if the spec document was modified
type_specific_fields.updated_spec_version: string — new spec version if updated, or empty string
type_specific_fields.updated_spec_path: string — file path to updated spec if updated, or empty string
type_specific_fields.ppm_action_required: boolean — whether PPM needs to notify affected SPMs of the change

interface_dispute.description: Two SPMs submit this jointly to SD when an interface conflict cannot be resolved directly. SD issues a binding_resolution in response. PPM is notified. PM is not involved unless the resolution itself is disputed. This is Fix 4 from Org Structure v2.0.
interface_dispute.destination: Written to {spm_a}_to_sd.json or {spm_b}_to_sd.json. Joint authorship recorded in the message. Notification to sd_inbox.json and ppm_inbox.json.

type_specific_fields.interface_id: string — the interface contract in dispute
type_specific_fields.spec_reference: string — file path or spec_release message_id of the relevant spec
type_specific_fields.spm_a_position: string — what spm_a believes the interface contract requires
type_specific_fields.spm_b_position: string — what spm_b believes the interface contract requires
type_specific_fields.conflict_description: string — precise description of the conflict. What they disagree on.
type_specific_fields.attempted_resolutions: array of strings — what both SPMs already tried before escalating
type_specific_fields.blocking_tasks: array of strings — task IDs blocked pending resolution

binding_resolution.description: SD's authoritative resolution of an interface_dispute between two SPMs. Both SPMs receive this. PPM is notified. The resolution is binding — neither SPM may deviate from it without a new spec_correction_request.
binding_resolution.destination: Written to sd_to_{spm_a}.json and sd_to_{spm_b}.json. Notifications to both SPM inboxes and ppm_inbox.json.

type_specific_fields.interface_id: string — the interface contract being resolved
type_specific_fields.resolution: string — the definitive statement of what the interface contract requires. This is the new truth.
type_specific_fields.rationale: string — full explanation referencing the original spec. Mandatory. Never empty.
type_specific_fields.spec_update_required: boolean — whether the spec document must be updated to reflect this resolution
type_specific_fields.updated_spec_version: string — new spec version if updated, or empty string
type_specific_fields.updated_spec_path: string — file path to updated spec, or empty string
type_specific_fields.effective_immediately: boolean — always true. Binding resolutions take effect on receipt.
type_specific_fields.affected_tasks: array of strings — task IDs that must be updated to reflect this resolution
